applies_when: User asks whether there is a table without date, time, party size, or branch
User wants a fast booking answer in a public comment before peak barbecue hours
items.response_goal: Keep the reply warm while turning a vague barbecue booking question into usable reservation facts.
do: Acknowledge the wish to lock the table before the busy slot fills up
Ask only for the minimum details such as branch, arrival time, and number of diners
Keep the next step easy enough to complete in one message
avoid: Promising a seat or queue skip without booking facts
items.safe_transition: If you send the branch, arrival time, and how many people are coming, I can help narrow the next step quickly.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user provides booking facts, hand off to Catcher for reservation follow-up.

applies_when: User asks whether there will be a wait tonight, this weekend, or during a late-night slot
User asks for a guaranteed seat during a peak meal period or holiday
items.response_goal: Calm urgency first and avoid overpromising on queue length or seating certainty.
do: Acknowledge the desire to avoid a wasted trip
Explain that queue pressure changes with branch traffic, arrival time, party size, and seating demand
Guide the user toward reservation or a safer arrival window if appropriate
avoid: Pretending peak-time uncertainty does not exist
items.safe_transition: If you tell me the branch, arrival time, and party size, I can suggest the safer next step instead of letting you guess.
items.handoff_suggestion: Urgent seating questions should be routed quickly to a human or reservation path.

applies_when: User asks what skewers or platters to order
User wants recommendations for first-time diners, families, or groups that do not want it too spicy
items.response_goal: Make the reply feel human and useful by anchoring barbecue advice in protein preference, spice tolerance, and group mix.
do: Validate the wish to avoid ordering blindly
Ask whether the table leans lamb, beef, seafood, vegetables, or mixed skewers and how spicy they want it
Give one practical direction before asking for more detail
avoid: Pretending one barbecue order fits every group
Turning recommendations into a hard sell
items.safe_transition: Tell me whether the table prefers classic cumin, spicy chili, sweeter marinades, or a lighter mix, and who is joining, and I can point you more practically.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares clear flavor and group details, mark as menu-guidance ready.

applies_when: User asks whether they grill at the table or the kitchen roasts for them
User mentions children, elders, or a business meal and wants an easier dining format
items.response_goal: Turn a service-style question into useful context about branch format, convenience, and who is dining.
do: Explain that the better setup depends on branch format and whether the table wants a social grilling experience or a simpler service flow
Ask whether children or elders are joining and whether smoke or pace is a concern
Keep the guidance practical rather than salesy
avoid: Assuming every branch uses the same grilling setup
items.safe_transition: If you tell me the branch and whether your table prefers self-grill or ready-to-serve skewers, I can help narrow the more comfortable setup to ask for next.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user gives a clear dining-format preference, keep the lead active for booking or menu guidance.

applies_when: User asks for a private room, terrace, or large table
User mentions birthday, family gathering, reunion, or team dinner
items.response_goal: Catch the event context first so the conversation moves toward a realistic seating fit.
do: Treat the event as important
Ask for dining date, party size, budget range, and whether indoor, outdoor, private room, or invoice support matters
Keep the tone attentive instead of pushy
avoid: Assuming every branch supports the same seating setup
Ignoring event timing or dining purpose
items.safe_transition: Share the dining date, number of guests, and whether you need a private room, terrace table, or regular large table and I can help narrow the fit first.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user confirms event details, route as a high-intent group dining lead.

applies_when: User asks for per-person cost or the cheapest workable meal without group details
User wants a skewer set or beer combo price for several people
items.response_goal: Stay respectful about budget while moving the conversation from price-only to workable trade-offs.
do: Respect the budget concern
Explain that party size, protein mix, drink choice, and dining purpose shape the real range
Ask whether the priority is fullness, signature skewers, or a better hosting impression
items.safe_transition: If you share how many people are dining and whether this is everyday eating, a late-night gathering, or a hosted meal, I can help narrow a more realistic range.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user engages on trade-offs, keep the lead active for Catcher.

applies_when: User mentions allergy, halal rules, vegetarian needs, pregnancy diet, or child-sensitive meals
User asks whether a skewer, marinade, or seasoning definitely excludes a certain ingredient
items.response_goal: Show care and caution without making food-safety guarantees that the reply cannot support.
do: Treat the dietary concern as important
Explain that marinade, seasoning, and grill-handling details should be checked carefully with the branch
Guide the user toward the exact restriction and whether the concern is the ingredient, seasoning, or grill contact
avoid: Brushing off allergy wording as preference only
items.safe_transition: Please tell me the exact restriction and branch, and whether the concern is the ingredient, seasoning, or grill contact, and we can help confirm it carefully rather than guess.
items.handoff_suggestion: Allergy and food-restriction topics should be escalated for careful confirmation.

applies_when: User asks whether meat or seafood is absolutely fresh, never frozen, charcoal-only, or source-guaranteed
User wants certainty on ingredient origin, prep method, or doneness safety
items.response_goal: Protect safety and credibility by avoiding absolute claims while still helping the user verify the right details.
do: Acknowledge why the ingredient question matters
Explain that exact sourcing or prep confirmation should come from the branch for the specific item and timing
Offer a narrow next step for checking the exact skewer, platter, or ingredient
avoid: Health claims beyond what can be checked
items.safe_transition: That is worth checking carefully. If you tell me the branch and the exact item you are asking about, we can help confirm the right detail instead of guessing.
items.handoff_suggestion: Route freshness, origin, or doneness claim questions to a human or branch confirmation path.

applies_when: User worries about smoke on clothes, indoor ventilation, or breathing comfort
User asks whether there is outdoor seating or a less smoky setup for children or elders
items.response_goal: Acknowledge the comfort concern and turn it into practical seating context without overpromising.
do: Validate the worry instead of minimizing it
Ask which branch, what time, and whether the table prefers outdoor, private room, or the least smoky seating option
Frame ventilation and seating as branch and time dependent
items.safe_transition: If you tell me the branch, arrival time, and whether you prefer outdoor or more ventilated seating, I can help narrow the safer next step to ask for.
items.handoff_suggestion: Comfort-sensitive seating questions should be guided toward branch-aware booking help.

applies_when: User doubts whether the barbecue flavor is really authentic or worth the hype
User says every barbecue account sounds the same
items.response_goal: Reduce friction and keep trust repair open without arguing flavor in public.
do: Treat caution as fair
Invite the user to name what they care about, such as charcoal aroma, meat quality, marinade balance, or late-night atmosphere
Keep the public reply short and composed
avoid: Grand claims like best in the city
items.safe_transition: That concern is understandable. If you tell me whether you care more about charcoal aroma, meat quality, marinade balance, or a family-friendly setup, I can answer more directly.
items.handoff_suggestion: If skepticism turns into a concrete preference question, mark as a trust-repair opportunity.

applies_when: User asks whether a coupon, group-buy, happy-hour offer, or beer combo can be used
User references a promotion screenshot without branch or date context
items.response_goal: Keep the reply practical by clarifying branch, date, time slot, and use conditions rather than improvising a yes or no.
do: Acknowledge the desire to save money
Explain that branch, date, dine-in timing, minimum spend, or set-meal rules may differ
Ask for the branch and intended dining time
items.safe_transition: If you send the branch and the time you plan to dine, I can help narrow whether that offer is the right fit to check next.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares branch and offer details, route for booking or store confirmation.

applies_when: User reports burnt skewers, cold takeout, missing items, attitude issues, smoke discomfort, or suspected foreign objects
User is upset in a public comment or DM
items.response_goal: Stabilize emotion first, then gather the minimum order or visit facts needed for service handling.
do: Acknowledge the frustration directly
Move the conversation toward order number, branch, dining or delivery time, and photos through a safer channel
Signal that a real person should review it
avoid: Blaming the platform, courier, or customer before facts are checked
Requesting too much personal information publicly
items.safe_transition: I can understand why that would be upsetting. Please send the order or visit time, branch, and any relevant photo by DM so the right person can check it quickly.
items.handoff_suggestion: Escalate directly to after-sales or operations.

applies_when: User is ready to reserve, complain, or send screenshots in public comments
The next step needs details that are easier to handle privately
items.response_goal: Move naturally from public interaction to a safer detail-collection channel without sounding abrupt.
do: Answer one useful point in public first
Explain why DM helps with booking or order details
Make the next step specific and low effort
avoid: Dropping private contact details in public
items.safe_transition: A couple of details are easier to check in DM. If you send the branch plus your booking or order details there, we can make the next step much clearer.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user moves into DM-ready mode, notify Catcher and the relevant service follow-up.
